novel_features.name: Net-investment year-over-year comparison
novel_features.description: See this year's FPI net flow next to the same period last year, delta computed automatically.
novel_features.rationale: NSDL's report page only shows one year at a time; comparing two years means loading two pages and subtracting by hand.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a request compares FPI flow across two adjacent years instead of listing raw rows.

novel_features.name: Sector rotation top movers
novel_features.description: Rank which sectors saw the biggest FPI inflow/outflow swing between the two most recent synced periods.
novel_features.rationale: NSDL's sector report shows one fortnight's absolute values only; ranking period-over-period deltas requires a local join across synced snapshots.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for 'what sector are FPIs rotating into/out of' instead of a single-period sector snapshot.

novel_features.name: Foreign investment limit utilization
novel_features.description: See what percentage of the regulatory FPI investment cap a sector or debt category has used.
novel_features.rationale: NSDL's limit-monitoring page shows raw caps only; computing %-of-cap requires joining synced limits against synced investment/AUC totals.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to flag sectors approaching a regulatory FPI cap, which can trigger a trading halt.

novel_features.name: Buying/selling streak detection
novel_features.description: See how many consecutive fortnights/years FPIs have been net buyers or sellers, and the most recent flip.
novel_features.rationale: Requires an ordered local scan of the full synced history for sign-change points — NSDL's raw table never computes this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for 'how many periods in a row' questions instead of scanning raw historical rows.

novel_features.name: AUC historical trend
novel_features.description: Track how a country's or category's share of assets under custody has changed across synced snapshots.
novel_features.rationale: NSDL's AUC report shows only the current snapshot; a trend view only exists because sync retains history NSDL discards.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this once at least two snapshots are synced to see custody-share direction, not a single-point value.

novel_features.name: Historical extremes
novel_features.description: Find the largest single-period FPI inflows or outflows across the full 1992-93-to-present series.
novel_features.rationale: A deterministic ORDER BY over 30+ years of synced history — no source ranks its own historical table by magnitude.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for 'biggest ever' / superlative questions about historical FPI flow.

novel_features.name: Net-investment trend
novel_features.description: See the rolling growth-rate and direction of FPI net flow over a window of recent periods.
novel_features.rationale: A rolling-window computation over synced net_investment rows in local SQLite; not available from any single NSDL page load.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for a derived growth-rate/direction view, not raw historical rows (use 'net-investment fy'/'cy'/'quarterly'/'latest' for those).

novel_features.name: NSDL/CDSL discrepancy flagging
novel_features.description: Compare NSDL's and CDSL's figures for the same date and flag any mismatch.
novel_features.rationale: A mechanical join/diff across two independently-synced sources for the same date — neither source ever compares itself against the other.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to cross-check the two Indian depositories' published figures for the same date.

narrative.headline: The full history of Foreign Portfolio Investor flows into India, synced locally and queryable offline — something neither NSDL nor CDSL offers today.
narrative.value_prop: NSDL and CDSL publish authoritative FPI investment data, but only as ASP.NET report pages with a client-side Excel export button — no API, no bulk historical download, no scriptable access. fpi-india syncs the full net-investment series back to 1992-93, plus AUC, sector rotation, trade data, and the FPI registry, into a local SQLite database you can query, trend, and pipe.

quickstart.comment: confirm the CLI can reach NSDL before syncing
pull the full historical FY series (1992-93-to-present) into local SQLite
see the synced historical equity flow series
compare this year's flow against last year, computed locally
see the current buying/selling streak, a computation no source page offers

troubleshoots.symptom: sync returns 0 rows for a report
troubleshoots.fix: NSDL occasionally changes a report's table id after a site update; run `fpi-india-pp-cli doctor --json` to confirm the page is reachable, then check for a generator update.

troubleshoots.symptom: cdsl commands return stale or missing dates
troubleshoots.fix: CDSL only publishes a rolling recent window of daily .xls snapshots (not deep history); older dates will not be available from CDSL even after sync.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI for historical or current Indian FPI investment flow analysis: net-investment trends, year-over-year and streak analysis, sector rotation, assets-under-custody trends, FPI registry lookups, and regulatory limit utilization. It is the right tool whenever a task needs NSDL/CDSL FPI data queried, trended, or compared rather than eyeballed from a report page.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for live/real-time trade execution or order placement — it is a read-only data and analytics tool.
Do not use this CLI for individual investor portfolio holdings or demat account data — NSDL/CDSL's FPI reports are aggregate market-level data, not account-level.
Do not use this CLI for forward-looking flow forecasts or investment advice — every command surfaces historical, already-published figures only.

recipes.title: Full historical equity flow series
recipes.explanation: Narrow a 30+ year series down to just the fields needed for a chart or note.

recipes.title: This fortnight vs last year, same fortnight
recipes.explanation: The Monday-morning commentary workflow: one command instead of loading two pages and subtracting by hand.

recipes.title: What sector is money rotating into
recipes.explanation: Ranks period-over-period sector deltas instead of a single period's absolute figures.

recipes.title: Is any sector near its FPI investment cap
recipes.explanation: Joins synced limits against synced investment totals to compute percent-of-cap used.

recipes.title: Biggest FPI outflow ever recorded
recipes.explanation: Deterministic ranking over the full synced history for journalist-style superlative questions.
